title: Diarrhea follow-up in GP
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA referral section supports clinician-entered safety-net, referral status, questions, and follow-up for qualified adult acute contexts without assigning urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

examination.text: Supports duration, stool pattern, urgency, incontinence, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, medicines, surgery, travel, family history, diet, and physical examination.
examination.rationale: The exact chronic-diarrhoea section supports interval duration, stool pattern, urgency, incontinence, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, medicines, surgery, travel, family history, diet, and clinician-performed examination without assigning cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports duration, stool pattern, urgency, incontinence, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, medicines, surgery, travel, family history, diet, and physical examination.

source.title: Guidelines for the investigation of chronic diarrhoea in adults: British Society of Gastroenterology, 3rd edition

investigations.text: Supports clinician review of existing blood, stool, FIT, endoscopy, imaging, coeliac, bile-acid, microscopic-colitis, and lactose-malabsorption investigations.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording already reviewed blood, stool, FIT, coeliac, endoscopy, imaging, bile-acid, microscopic-colitis, and lactose-malabsorption context without ordering tests.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician review of existing blood, stool, FIT, endoscopy, imaging, coeliac, bile-acid, microscopic-colitis, and lactose-malabsorption investigations.

source.title: Guidelines for the investigation of chronic diarrhoea in adults: British Society of Gastroenterology, 3rd edition

source_limitations: Current stool frequency and form, hydration, bleeding, travel, examination, cause, diagnosis, test, treatment, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
